title: Orange Room
background: Orange Room started as a visual experiment for Layer Eleven, a space to explore how structure, motion, and contrast could speak without words. The brief was intentionally abstract. The execution had to be precise. Built from scratch with a custom scroll system, layered transitions, and a minimalist grid, the site blends cinematic visuals with frontend discipline.
review: Working with Juno felt more like teaming up with a creative partner than hiring a developer. From the first conversation, he understood the energy and mood we were aiming for. He asked the right questions, explored visual directions with intent, and delivered a build that felt as thoughtful as it was bold.

Every detail, from scroll behavior to motion timing, was carefully considered. Juno brought clarity to a concept that had only lived in loose sketches and moodboards. He helped us tell a story through interface and interaction. The result is something that not only represents who we are, but inspires how we build from here.

title: Future School
background: Future School was built to make a learning platform feel less like software and more like a living system. We designed an editorial interface with bold pacing, layered color, and a classroom-inspired motion language that could stay clear under heavy content. The result balanced structure and optimism without flattening the brand into something generic.
review: The project needed a system that could scale across marketing, product storytelling, and curriculum pages without losing personality. Juno translated that into a visual language that felt immediate and usable from day one.
The final build gave us clarity, pace, and a stronger point of view. It feels polished, but not sterile, which is exactly where we wanted to land.

title: Sweetbones
background: Sweetbones leaned into contrast: tactile visuals, sharp typography, and motion that felt slightly too alive to be safe. The site was framed as a brand experience first and a portfolio second, so every section had to feel staged, cinematic, and easy to navigate at the same time.
review: Juno gave the project a stronger attitude without making it harder to use. The design has edge, but the structure stays clean, which made the whole experience feel more expensive than the brief started with.
What stood out most was the discipline behind the visuals. Nothing was loud for the sake of it. Every motion cue and layout shift had a reason.
